decisions.decision: Add one dedicated success-path test in `BayesMarketApiAuthRateLimitTests` that freezes the full accepted comment-post HTTP envelope: `201`, the `comment` object, and the `meta` object. Assert stable comment fields exactly and generated fields semantically or by tie-out rather than hard-coded literal values.
alternatives_rejected: Assert only `201` plus a minimal subset of stable comment fields.
Duplicate the shared breadth-loop header check without route-specific payload assertions.

decisions.decision: Keep the test centered on the immediate POST response plus `assert_rate_limit_headers(...)`, but add a light direct-state tie-out to `server.COMMENTS` and `server.MARKET_COMMENT_SEQUENCES` so generated values like `commentId`, `createdAt`, and `seq` are anchored without expanding into a follow-up comments-list read.
alternatives_rejected: Expand the test into a `GET /v1/markets/{id}/comments` end-to-end flow.
Skip any persistence tie-out and only assert that generated fields are present.

decisions.decision: Reuse the existing limiter-enabled, one-shot setup and `comment_post_with_headers(...)` helper for a fresh first accepted comment on `m1`, so the standalone test adds route-local contract coverage instead of inventing a second-comment, normalization-specific, or alternate quota scenario.
alternatives_rejected: Post a second comment to pin `seq == 2`.
Change the helper surface to cover a different scenario in this step.

decisions.decision: Keep this subtask success-path only: do not fold idempotency replay/conflict or missing-agent and no-header negative cases into the new test, because those belong to later route-specific coverage rather than this first-class accepted-response seam.
alternatives_rejected: Expand the dedicated test to cover `idempotencyKey` replay and conflict behavior.
Start the route-specific negative auth and disabled-limiter matrix for comment-post in the same patch.

steps.task: Inspect the existing comment-post helper methods, the shared `assert_rate_limit_headers(...)` helper, and the current breadth success test in `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py` so the new standalone test lands in `BayesMarketApiAuthRateLimitTests` without duplicating unrelated limiter coverage.

Add a dedicated comment-post success test that sets `RATE_LIMIT_PER_MIN` to a positive value, submits one valid `POST /v1/markets/m1/comments` with an explicit `X-Bayes-Agent-Id`, asserts `201`, verifies the accepted `comment` and `meta` response contract, and checks the shared rate-limit headers for the first successful write.

Tie the generated response fields back to in-memory persisted state with lightweight assertions against `server.COMMENTS` and `server.MARKET_COMMENT_SEQUENCES`, confirming the returned `commentId`, `createdAt`, and `seq` match the stored accepted comment without adding a separate read-path test.

Run the focused `BayesMarketApiAuthRateLimitTests` coverage for the new comment-post success case, review the diff to confirm the change stays tests-only, and commit the worktree with the requested message.
